core_mechanism: Bosonization: a change of variables that recasts the collective excitations of a system of strongly interacting particles as decoupled harmonic wave (density-fluctuation) modes, turning an intractable interacting problem into an analytically solvable linear one, combined with factorizing the dynamics into independent sub-sectors of which one is mapped to collective modes and the other solved exactly.
brief_reason: The abstract states the method "involves bosonization of the charge degrees of freedom, while the spin excitations are treated exactly," i.e. it maps one sector's collective excitations onto harmonic wave modes and factorizes the dynamics into independent sectors to obtain analytic spectral functions across a broad energy range.
